name: Sourdough Stuffed Trout
recipeIngredient: 1 small loaf sourdough or crusty Italian bread
1/2 cup Thinly sliced green onions (including tops)
6 Whole cleaned trout (each about 12 inches long)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Cut the bread into 1/2-inch cubes and spread them in a single layer on a baking sheet. Bake, stirring occasionally, until the cubes are dry and crisp (about 10 minutes). Remove from the oven and transfer the cubes to a bowl.
3. Combine the bread cubes with parsley, salt, pepper, green onion tops, chopped green pepper, white wine, and 2 tablespoons of butter in the bowl. Mix lightly.
4. Wipe the trout with a damp cloth, both inside and outside the cavities.
5. Brush the cavities with some of the remaining butter and sprinkle lightly with salt and pepper.
6. Stuff the cavities loosely with the bread mixture, dividing it evenly among the fish. Secure the edges together or sew with heavy thread.
7. Arrange the fish side by side in a greased shallow baking pan (use two pans if necessary to hold all fish at once).
8. Drizzle any remaining butter evenly over the tops of the fish.
9. Bake the fish, uncovered, until it flakes readily when poked in the thickest portion with a fork. For a 1-inch thick fish, bake for about 10 minutes on one side and 5 minutes on the other.
